trustRules.label: foundational math/camera/transform — breaking changes break everything
render hot path, zero allocations after warmup
has allocations, verify not on hot path
fork of upstream NoZ — additive changes only, must log in maintenance.md

zero-alloc.rule: Zero GPU/heap allocations on render hot path after warmup
zero-alloc.reason: 16ms frame budget (60 FPS). Any CreateMesh, CreateTexture, CreateDepthTexture, or heap allocation in Update/Draw risks frame drops.

no-n-plus-one-uploads.rule: No per-object uniform uploads in a loop
no-n-plus-one-uploads.reason: Uploading uniforms one object at a time in a draw loop causes N+1 GPU calls. Use globals snapshot system or batch into a single buffer update.

no-rendering-deps.rule: YesZ.Core must not reference YesZ.Rendering or any GPU/graphics types
no-rendering-deps.reason: Core is the foundational math layer — it must remain pure and testable without GPU.

thread-safe.reason: Platform/driver layer may be called from multiple threads. Shared state must be synchronized.

additive-only.rule: Only additive changes to NoZ fork
additive-only.reason: Modifying existing NoZ behavior breaks upstream merge compatibility. Extend, don't modify.

managed-collections-in-hot-path.bad: Dictionary<K,V>, List<T>, or any managed collection in per-frame render code
managed-collections-in-hot-path.good: Flat array with const capacity + index-based access, or pre-allocated pools
managed-collections-in-hot-path.why: Managed collections allocate on the heap and trigger GC in per-frame code paths.

linq-in-hot-path.bad: LINQ queries (.Select, .Where, .ToArray, etc.) in per-frame render code
linq-in-hot-path.why: Every LINQ call allocates an iterator and closure on the heap.

gpu-alloc-in-draw.bad: CreateMesh, CreateTexture, CreateDepthTexture, new buffer allocations inside Update/Draw/Render
gpu-alloc-in-draw.good: Pre-allocate all GPU resources at scene load or warmup; reuse via pools
gpu-alloc-in-draw.why: GPU allocations during rendering cause frame stalls and exceed the 16ms budget.

breaking-noz-change.bad: Modifying existing NoZ method signatures, removing methods, changing behavior
breaking-noz-change.good: Add new methods/overloads, extend via partial classes or new types
breaking-noz-change.why: Breaking changes to the NoZ fork make upstream merges painful or impossible.

globals-snapshot.name: Globals snapshot
globals-snapshot.description: Per-transform data uploaded via NoZ globals snapshot system (max 64 per frame). Avoids per-object uniform upload loops.

depth-texture-array.name: Depth texture array
depth-texture-array.description: Shadow map cascades stored in a texture array, indexed by cascade level in the shader.

cascade-shadow-map.name: Cascaded shadow map
cascade-shadow-map.description: Multiple shadow map cascades at different resolutions/ranges for directional lights.

batch-uniform-upload.name: Batch uniform upload
batch-uniform-upload.description: Material or per-object uniforms batched into a single buffer update instead of N individual uploads.
